footer.tagline: AI coworkers, process automation, and internal systems for business owners. Part of the NLO Ecosystem.

hero.badge: AI Coworkers · Process Automation · Internal Systems
hero.subtitle: AI agents and automations replace the routine work of 2-4 employees. They run 24/7, never call in sick, never ask for a raise. You keep the team that actually drives revenue.

cards.title: Payroll grows, profit doesn't
cards.desc: You hire 3 more managers — customer throughput stays the same. Salaries go up, margins go down. New hires = not growth, just more costs.

cards.title: Support is a daily bottleneck
cards.desc: Customers wait hours for replies. Your support team writes the same answer a hundred times a day. New leads slip through, reviews tank.

cards.title: Processes live in Natalie's head
cards.desc: Excel, Telegram, and the memory of that one 'key' employee. Kostya gets sick — sales stop. Oleh quits — customers don't know where to write.

solution.title: AI doesn't replace strong people — it takes away work you shouldn't be paying them for
solution.p1: I combine AI agents (Claude, GPT), no-code automation (n8n, Zapier, Make), and custom systems. Things that needed a CTO and 3 devs a year ago now ship in 2-3 weeks with a guaranteed result.
solution.p2: This isn't 'magic AI'. It's engineering discipline: integrations, tests, monitoring, human-in-the-loop. Your team doesn't go away — it works at higher output, on things that actually move revenue.
bullets: AI coworkers for support, sales, research — 24/7, no sick days
Automation: orders, reports, CRM sync, reconciliation
Internal systems: dashboards, client portals, your own CRM
Integrations between your existing stack (CRM, accounting, Telegram, email, WhatsApp)
Ship in 2-4 weeks. Code and configuration are yours.

services.title: What I do
services.subtitle: Three directions that make your business earn more with a smaller team.

ai-agents.title: AI Coworkers
ai-agents.tagline: Virtual employees on Claude/GPT instead of support reps, SDRs, researchers. 24/7, no PTO, 80% cheaper than one salary.

automation.title: Process Automation
automation.tagline: CRM, ETL, reports, system sync. Your team stops copying Excel and starts driving revenue.

systems.title: Internal Systems
systems.tagline: Custom CRM, dashboards, client portals. Built for your business — instead of $500/mo on software that doesn't fit.

process.title: How I work
process.subtitle: Transparent at every step. You see savings weekly instead of waiting for a 'big reveal' in six months.

steps.title: Process audit
steps.desc: 30 minutes, free. I map where routine is eating your team and what it costs. Honest take on where AI helps and where it doesn't.

steps.title: Proposal with a number
steps.desc: Fixed scope, timeline, and expected savings ($/month or hours/week). No surprise add-ons mid-project.

steps.title: Ship in 2-4 weeks
steps.desc: Weekly demos. You test on real customers, give feedback, I polish. No surprises at the end.

steps.title: Gradual rollout
steps.desc: 5% traffic → 30% → 100%. I monitor quality, human-in-the-loop on complex cases. Your team learns to operate.

steps.title: Scale
steps.desc: 2 weeks free support. Then either a monitoring retainer for improvements, or handover to your team. Your call.

proof.title: What I actually automate in real businesses
proof.subtitle: Typical results from my clients. Numbers are project averages — exact savings depend on your business (I calculate during the audit).

support.title: AI Support Agent
support.desc: Replaces 2 front-line support reps. CSAT 90%+, 24/7, 3-second response time. Escalates complex cases to humans.

sales.title: Sales Qualifier
sales.desc: AI processes every form lead, calls, qualifies, hands only hot ones to reps. SDRs stop wasting time on junk leads.

orders.title: Order automation
orders.desc: Syncs orders across Shopify/accounting/Telegram chats. Status updates, SMS, receipts — zero manual copying. Fewer errors.

dashboard.title: Real-time dashboard
dashboard.desc: Sales, conversion, margin by category — live. Spot issues on Monday, not Friday. Analyst works on strategy, not reports.

research.title: AI research agent
research.desc: Pulls prospects by your criteria from LinkedIn, Google, directories. Instead of hiring a research rep — $50/mo in API.

ecosystem.title: Need more than just automation?
ecosystem.subtitle: NLO Coding is part of a five-product AI ecosystem. Strategy, brand, content, finance — all for your business, under one roof. Engage me and you get access to everything.

founder.title: Oleksii — the one who understands business
founder.subtitle: 12+ years in marketing, sales, and consulting. 200+ clients taken through business transformation. Author of the Bottleneck methodology. Founder of Sales Titans.
founder.p: I'm not from IT — I'm from business. That's why I understand how automation has to fit into your processes, not the other way around. I write the code myself and run the project as a partner — because I don't trust that to people who never looked at the client's numbers.

items.a: From $2K for a single automation to $10-15K for a full AI coworker with integrations. Payback in 1-3 months — I show you the math in your numbers during the audit. If savings don't 3x the cost, I don't take the project.

items.q: What if the AI messes up with a customer?
items.a: Human-in-the-loop. AI handles 70-90% of simple requests. Anything complex or sensitive — it escalates to a human. I review 100% of responses in week one, a sample after. Errors are fixed immediately.

items.q: Won't I lose the human voice of my brand?
items.a: AI responds in your tone — I feed it your old emails, tickets, conversations. Customers often don't realize they're talking to AI, because answers are fast and on-point. Anything complex — always a human.

items.q: What do I tell the employees we're replacing?
items.a: Usually no one gets laid off. People move to work that actually drives revenue — sales, customer growth, strategy. AI takes the routine nobody wanted in the first place.

items.q: What if my business is offline (shop, restaurant, salon)?
items.a: Works with any. WhatsApp/Telegram bots for bookings, CRM for returning customers, auto-reports per shift. You don't need a website — just digital-first communication.

items.q: What if I don't know where to start?
items.a: 30 minutes free — I audit your business, show you 2-3 highest-ROI wins, and tell you honestly what to do first. Even if you don't become a client, the audit is yours to keep.

items.q: Who owns the code and setup?
items.a: You. Always. Code in your GitHub, API keys yours, prompts and settings yours. You can continue with anyone or on your own. Zero vendor lock-in.

finalCta.title: 30 minutes — and you know how much you can save
finalCta.subtitle: Free audit of your processes. I'll show you 2-3 highest-ROI wins. Even if you don't become a client, the audit is yours.

telegram.desc: Direct conversation with Oleksii. The fastest path.

form.desc: Tell me briefly — I'll reply within 24 hours.

calendly.desc: 30 minutes, free. I explore your idea honestly.

targetLanding.offer: Save $3-8K/month on payroll. In 3 weeks — or your deposit back.

meta.title: SaaS MVP in 2-4 weeks — NLO Coding
meta.description: Idea to shipped product in a month. Next.js + Supabase + Stripe + Claude API. You own the code from day one.

hero.title: Ship an MVP in 2-4 weeks
hero.subtitle: For founders who want to validate a hypothesis, not wait quarters. Full cycle: auth, payments, deploy — and first real users by end of month.

pains.title: Investors want a live product
pains.desc: Slide decks don't work anymore. Show working software — raise the round. Show Figma — no.

pains.title: A team shouldn't cost like a mortgage
pains.desc: At hypothesis stage you can't afford $30K/mo on senior devs. But $0 isn't an option either.

pains.title: Time to first feedback is critical
pains.desc: The longer you're without real users, the higher the chance you're building the wrong thing. MVP isn't 'better later.' It's 'needed yesterday.'

included: Idea audit + feature prioritization
Architecture (DB, API, integrations)
UI design in Figma + design system
Frontend (Next.js + Tailwind)
Backend (Supabase + Row-Level Security)
Auth (email + OAuth: Google, GitHub)
Payments (Stripe: subscriptions, one-time, trial)
Email (transactional + marketing)
Deploy on your domain (Vercel)
Monitoring and analytics (PostHog)
Documentation + team onboarding
2 weeks of free post-launch support

timeline.title: Week 1
timeline.desc: Discovery deep-dive, architecture, wireframes, design system, scope lock.

timeline.title: Week 2-3
timeline.desc: Core build: auth, DB, key features, payments. Weekly demos.

timeline.title: Week 4
timeline.desc: Polish, tests, deploy, monitoring, first users. Handover.

proof.desc: Full SaaS for AI-driven marketing strategies. 15-section documents, 7-phase dialogue, auth, subscription, export. Idea to launch — 3 weeks.

faq.q: What if I don't know exactly what I want yet?
faq.a: That's normal. The Discovery Call is literally for this. 30 minutes to extract the essence, formulate the MVP scope, and tell you honestly whether to start or spend another week thinking.

faq.q: What if I need mobile apps?
faq.a: A PWA (installable web app) is part of the standard MVP. Native iOS/Android — separate phase after market validation.

faq.q: How do we measure MVP success?
faq.a: Discovery Call — I agree on metrics with you before start: first 10 paying users, N MAU, hypothesis validation, etc. It's not 'a product,' it's 'a product that answers a question.'

faq.q: What if we need to change things along the way?
faq.a: Scope is fixed — but within scope, you control priorities weekly. If you need something outside scope, I add it to Phase 2 (post-MVP) or we discuss separately.

meta.title: Business Process Automation — NLO Coding
meta.description: Give your team back 20-40 hours per week. CRM, reports, orders, integrations — no manual work, ready in 2-3 weeks.

hero.title: Your team is copying data
hero.subtitle: Every hour spent moving data from Excel to CRM is an hour your manager isn't selling. I automate routine in 2-3 weeks. Get 20-40 team hours back per week — or your deposit back.

pains.title: Your managers are data couriers
pains.desc: Excel → CRM → Telegram → accounting → back to Excel. Errors, duplicates, delays. You're paying salaries for copy-paste.

pains.title: Every system is its own island
pains.desc: CRM can't see accounting. Accounting can't see inventory. Inventory can't see orders. You can't see anything in real time.

pains.title: Last week's report was outdated yesterday
pains.desc: By the time your analyst finishes the Excel report, you've already made decisions on stale data. Your competitor checked a dashboard this morning and moved faster.

included: Process audit (30 min free) + ROI calculation
Process map: what I automate, what stays with people
Workflows in n8n/Make/Zapier — or custom, if needed
Integrations with CRM, accounting, email, Telegram, WhatsApp
Real-time dashboard (replaces Excel reports)
Auto-notifications: orders, statuses, failures
Training one person on your team (keymaster, 2-3 hours)
Documentation: what to do when something breaks
2 weeks of free monitoring

timeline.title: Week 1
timeline.desc: Process audit. Pick the top 3 by ROI. Lock scope + proposal with a number.

timeline.title: Week 2
timeline.desc: Build workflows + test on real data. Train the keymaster.

timeline.title: Week 3+
timeline.desc: Remaining processes by priority. Monitoring + fine-tune. Handover.

proof.desc: 5 SaaS products synced into one ecosystem: shared database, automatic referrals, unified partner dashboard. Orders, payouts, analytics — all running without manual work. Built in 3 weeks.

faq.q: Do we need a developer to maintain this?
faq.a: No. Automations on n8n/Make can be maintained by someone with no coding skills. I train one person on your team as the keymaster — 2-3 hours.

faq.q: What if our business process changes?
faq.a: 80% of changes — adding a step or a condition — take minutes. More complex ones: 30-min discovery + fix in a day or two. Workflows are modular, not monolithic.

faq.q: How much will we actually save?
faq.a: Typically: 20-40 team hours per week. That's $2-5K/month in payroll. Plus fewer errors — harder to quantify, but critical for customer experience.

faq.q: We have legacy software (old CRM, accounting) — does it work?
faq.a: Almost always yes. If it has an API or at least CSV export, I can integrate. Even Excel via OneDrive/Google Drive works. I check during the audit.

meta.title: AI Coworkers for Business — NLO Coding
meta.description: Replace routine roles with AI agents: support, sales, research. Running 24/7, costing 10x less than a salary.

hero.title: Paying salaries for routine?
hero.subtitle: AI coworkers answer customers, qualify leads, find new prospects, prepare reports. 24/7, no sick days, no 'I'm putting in my two weeks.' Up and running in 2-3 weeks — or your deposit back.

pains.title: Support gives the same answer 100 times a day
pains.desc: Customer asks 'where's my order?' — rep checks CRM, copies the status, sends it. 100 times. Every day. For $800/month.

pains.title: Sales spends 60% of their time on people who won't buy
pains.desc: A 'just checking the price' inquiry, a bot submission, a 'let me think about it.' Your rep treats them all the same — and can't get to the ones who are actually ready.

pains.title: You scale by hiring, not by building systems
pains.desc: +50 customers = +1 rep. Always. That's a linear model — AI breaks it: the same agent handles 10 and 10,000 requests per month at the same cost.

included: Audit: which roles can be delegated to AI (30 min free)
Agent design: role, instructions, constraints, brand tone
Knowledge base from your data: FAQs, pricing, policies, scripts
Integrations: CRM, Telegram, WhatsApp, email, website
Human-in-the-loop: complex or sensitive cases → escalation to a human
Voice extraction: the agent speaks in your tone, not like ChatGPT
Quality monitoring: 100% review in the first week
Analytics: conversations, CSAT, escalation rate, ROI
Team training: how to work alongside an AI colleague
2 weeks of support with daily monitoring

timeline.title: Week 1
timeline.desc: Role audit + agent design. Build the knowledge base. Test on 10 real requests.

timeline.title: Week 2
timeline.desc: Soft launch on 5-10% of traffic. Daily review of every response. Prompt fine-tuning.

timeline.title: Week 3
timeline.desc: Rollout to 100%. Team is trained. Handover + monitoring.

proof.desc: AI agents that write scripts, create posts for 9+ platforms, generate videos. One trigger — 30 content pieces. Serving multiple clients simultaneously with zero coordination. Replaced 2 content managers.

faq.q: What if the AI says something wrong to a customer?
faq.a: Human-in-the-loop. Anything complex or unfamiliar gets escalated to a human. I monitor 100% of responses in week one, then a sample after. Errors are fixed immediately.

faq.q: Won't customers be offended they're talking to a bot?
faq.a: 90% of customers don't realize they're talking to AI — because responses are fast, relevant, and in the right tone. Those who do notice appreciate not waiting 3 hours.

faq.q: What if I want to replace a specific role?
faq.a: I start with an audit of that exact role: how many hours go where, what repeats, where errors happen. Typically 60-80% of one person's workload can be fully automated.

faq.q: How much does it cost to run an AI agent after launch?
faq.a: API (Claude/OpenAI) — $50-300/month depending on volume. Hosting — $20-50/month. Total — 5-10x cheaper than a salary for the same role.

meta.title: About NLO Coding — who I am and how I work
meta.description: Business architect + AI = process automation for business owners. 200+ consultations, Bottleneck methodology, 5 SaaS in a year.

hero.title: I'm not from IT.
hero.subtitle: For 12+ years I built sales teams, marketing strategies, and CRM systems. Then I taught AI to do it with minimal human involvement. NLO Coding is building business systems by someone who understands P&L, not just code.

philosophy.title: Why this works
philosophy.p1: Most IT companies don't understand their client's business. They ask for a spec — and you don't know what to write. I start with an audit: where's the money, where are the leaks, what can be automated — and only then do I write code.
philosophy.p2: That's why I don't drag projects for years, don't sell retainers with minimal results, don't hide configurations behind lock-in. Fast, transparent, all access is yours.
philosophy.p3: The Bottleneck methodology is my own framework. I find the narrowest point in your business — where the most revenue is slipping through — and fix that first. Not 'do everything at once,' but 'do one thing and see results within a week.'

founder.title: Oleksii Nikolaichuk
founder.p1: 12+ years: sales rep → marketer → C-level (CMO, COO, R&D director) → own consultancy Bottle Neck → Sales Titans (building sales teams) → Brand Exorcist (personal consulting). 200+ one-on-one consultations, 95% satisfied clients.
founder.p2: During COVID built 20-30+ websites without developers and mastered 80+ no-code tools. Author of the Bottleneck methodology (business bottleneck diagnosis) and the signature strategic session — 6 hours of work replacing months with an agency.
founder.p3: In 2024 started experimenting with AI-accelerated development — n8n → Claude Code → Vibe Coding. In 2 months shipped 5 SaaS products in the NLO Ecosystem. NLO Coding is the natural extension: apply the same approach to client work. I write the code myself, run projects as a partner. Currently based in Setubal, Portugal.

ecosystem.subtitle: Each one built using the same approach I offer to clients. Live, working, with real users.
